questions.question: Who was the man described as perfect and upright in Job 1:1?
questions.correct_answer: Job

questions.question: How many sons and daughters did Job have in Job 1:2?
questions.correct_answer: Seven sons and three daughters

questions.question: How many sheep did Job own in Job 1:3?
questions.correct_answer: Seven thousand

questions.question: What does Job 1:4 say?
questions.correct_answer: And his sons went and feasted in their houses, every one his day; and sent and called for their three sisters to eat and to drink with them.
options: And his sons went and feasted in their houses, every one his day; and sent and called for their three sisters to eat and to drink with them.

questions.question: What did Job offer for his children in Job 1:5?
questions.correct_answer: Burnt offerings

questions.question: Who came among the sons of God in Job 1:6?
questions.correct_answer: Satan

questions.question: What does Job 1:7 say?
questions.correct_answer: And the LORD said unto Satan, Whence comest thou? Then Satan answered the LORD, and said, From going to and fro in the earth, and from walking up and down in it.
options: And the LORD said unto Satan, Whence comest thou? Then Satan answered the LORD, and said, From going to and fro in the earth, and from walking up and down in it.

questions.question: What does Job 1:8 say?
questions.correct_answer: And the LORD said unto Satan, Hast thou considered my servant Job, that there is none like him in the earth, a perfect and an upright man, one that feareth God, and escheweth evil?
options: And the LORD said unto Satan, Hast thou considered my servant Job, that there is none like him in the earth, a perfect and an upright man, one that feareth God, and escheweth evil?

questions.question: What question did Satan ask about Job in Job 1:9?
questions.correct_answer: Doth Job fear God for nought?
options: Doth Job fear God for nought?

questions.question: What had God made about Job and his house in Job 1:10?
questions.correct_answer: An hedge

questions.question: What did Satan say Job would do if he lost everything in Job 1:11?
questions.correct_answer: Curse thee to thy face

questions.question: What did God say was in Satan's power in Job 1:12?
questions.correct_answer: All that he hath

questions.question: What does Job 1:13 say?
questions.correct_answer: ¶ And there was a day when his sons and his daughters were eating and drinking wine in their eldest brother’s house:
options: ¶ And there was a day when his sons and his daughters were eating and drinking wine in their eldest brother’s house:

questions.question: What does Job 1:14 say?
questions.correct_answer: And there came a messenger unto Job, and said, The oxen were plowing, and the asses feeding beside them:
options: And there came a messenger unto Job, and said, The oxen were plowing, and the asses feeding beside them:

questions.question: What does Job 1:15 say?
questions.correct_answer: And the Sabeans fell upon them, and took them away; yea, they have slain the servants with the edge of the sword; and I only am escaped alone to tell thee.
options: And the Sabeans fell upon them, and took them away; yea, they have slain the servants with the edge of the sword; and I only am escaped alone to tell thee.

questions.question: What does Job 1:16 say?
questions.correct_answer: While he was yet speaking, there came also another, and said, The fire of God is fallen from heaven, and hath burned up the sheep, and the servants, and consumed them; and I only am escaped alone to tell thee.
options: While he was yet speaking, there came also another, and said, The fire of God is fallen from heaven, and hath burned up the sheep, and the servants, and consumed them; and I only am escaped alone to tell thee.

questions.question: What does Job 1:17 say?
questions.correct_answer: While he was yet speaking, there came also another, and said, The Chaldeans made out three bands, and fell upon the camels, and have carried them away, yea, and slain the servants with the edge of the sword; and I only am escaped alone to tell thee.
options: While he was yet speaking, there came also another, and said, The Chaldeans made out three bands, and fell upon the camels, and have carried them away, yea, and slain the servants with the edge of the sword; and I only am escaped alone to tell thee.

questions.question: What does Job 1:18 say?
questions.correct_answer: While he was yet speaking, there came also another, and said, Thy sons and thy daughters were eating and drinking wine in their eldest brother’s house:
options: While he was yet speaking, there came also another, and said, Thy sons and thy daughters were eating and drinking wine in their eldest brother’s house:

questions.question: What does Job 1:19 say?
questions.correct_answer: And, behold, there came a great wind from the wilderness, and smote the four corners of the house, and it fell upon the young men, and they are dead; and I only am escaped alone to tell thee.
options: And, behold, there came a great wind from the wilderness, and smote the four corners of the house, and it fell upon the young men, and they are dead; and I only am escaped alone to tell thee.

questions.question: What does Job 1:20 say?
questions.correct_answer: Then Job arose, and rent his mantle, and shaved his head, and fell down upon the ground, and worshipped,
options: Then Job arose, and rent his mantle, and shaved his head, and fell down upon the ground, and worshipped,

questions.question: What does Job 1:21 say?
questions.correct_answer: And said, Naked came I out of my mother’s womb, and naked shall I return thither: the LORD gave, and the LORD hath taken away; blessed be the name of the LORD.
options: And said, Naked came I out of my mother’s womb, and naked shall I return thither: the LORD gave, and the LORD hath taken away; blessed be the name of the LORD.

questions.question: How did Job respond to his suffering in Job 1:22?
questions.correct_answer: He sinned not nor charged God foolishly
options: He sinned not nor charged God foolishly

questions.question: Who came among the sons of God in Job 2:1?
questions.correct_answer: Satan

questions.question: What does Job 2:2 say?
questions.correct_answer: And the LORD said unto Satan, From whence comest thou? And Satan answered the LORD, and said, From going to and fro in the earth, and from walking up and down in it.
options: And the LORD said unto Satan, From whence comest thou? And Satan answered the LORD, and said, From going to and fro in the earth, and from walking up and down in it.

questions.question: What does Job 2:3 say?
questions.correct_answer: And the LORD said unto Satan, Hast thou considered my servant Job, that there is none like him in the earth, a perfect and an upright man, one that feareth God, and escheweth evil? and still he holdeth fast his integrity, although thou movedst me against him, to destroy him without cause.
options: And the LORD said unto Satan, Hast thou considered my servant Job, that there is none like him in the earth, a perfect and an upright man, one that feareth God, and escheweth evil? and still he holdeth fast his integrity, although thou movedst me against him, to destroy him without cause.

questions.question: What does Job 2:4 say?
questions.correct_answer: And Satan answered the LORD, and said, Skin for skin, yea, all that a man hath will he give for his life.
options: And Satan answered the LORD, and said, Skin for skin, yea, all that a man hath will he give for his life.

questions.question: What did Satan say Job would do if he lost everything in Job 2:5?
questions.correct_answer: Curse thee to thy face

questions.question: What command did God give Satan regarding Job in Job 2:6?
questions.correct_answer: Save his life

questions.question: What did Satan afflict Job with in Job 2:7?
questions.correct_answer: Sore boils

questions.question: What did Job take to scrape himself in Job 2:8?
questions.correct_answer: A potsherd

questions.question: What does Job 2:9 say?
questions.correct_answer: ¶ Then said his wife unto him, Dost thou still retain thine integrity? curse God, and die.
options: ¶ Then said his wife unto him, Dost thou still retain thine integrity? curse God, and die.

questions.question: What did Job say about receiving good and evil from God in Job 2:10?
questions.correct_answer: Shall we receive good at the hand of God, and shall we not receive evil?
options: Shall we receive good at the hand of God, and shall we not receive evil?

questions.question: What does Job 2:11 say?
questions.correct_answer: ¶ Now when Job’s three friends heard of all this evil that was come upon him, they came every one from his own place; Eliphaz the Temanite, and Bildad the Shuhite, and Zophar the Naamathite: for they had made an appointment together to come to mourn with him and to comfort him.
options: ¶ Now when Job’s three friends heard of all this evil that was come upon him, they came every one from his own place; Eliphaz the Temanite, and Bildad the Shuhite, and Zophar the Naamathite: for they had made an appointment together to come to mourn with him and to comfort him.

questions.question: What does Job 2:12 say?
questions.correct_answer: And when they lifted up their eyes afar off, and knew him not, they lifted up their voice, and wept; and they rent every one his mantle, and sprinkled dust upon their heads toward heaven.
options: And when they lifted up their eyes afar off, and knew him not, they lifted up their voice, and wept; and they rent every one his mantle, and sprinkled dust upon their heads toward heaven.

questions.question: How long did Job's friends sit with him in Job 2:13?
questions.correct_answer: Seven days and seven nights

questions.question: What did Job do to the day of his birth in Job 3:1?
questions.correct_answer: Cursed it

questions.question: What does Job 3:2 say?
questions.correct_answer: And Job spake, and said,

questions.question: What does Job 3:3 say?
questions.correct_answer: Let the day perish wherein I was born, and the night in which it was said, There is a man child conceived.
options: Let the day perish wherein I was born, and the night in which it was said, There is a man child conceived.

questions.question: What did Job wish for the day of his birth in Job 3:4?
questions.correct_answer: Let that day be darkness

questions.question: What two things did Job say should stain the day of his birth in Job 3:5?
questions.correct_answer: Darkness and the shadow of death
options: Darkness and the shadow of death

questions.question: What does Job 3:6 say?
questions.correct_answer: As for that night, let darkness seize upon it; let it not be joined unto the days of the year, let it not come into the number of the months.
options: As for that night, let darkness seize upon it; let it not be joined unto the days of the year, let it not come into the number of the months.

questions.question: What does Job 3:7 say?
questions.correct_answer: Lo, let that night be solitary, let no joyful voice come therein.
options: Lo, let that night be solitary, let no joyful voice come therein.

questions.question: What does Job 3:8 say?
questions.correct_answer: Let them curse it that curse the day, who are ready to raise up their mourning.
options: Let them curse it that curse the day, who are ready to raise up their mourning.

questions.question: What did Job say should happen to the stars of that day in Job 3:9?
questions.correct_answer: They should be dark

questions.question: What does Job 3:10 say?
questions.correct_answer: Because it shut not up the doors of my mother’s womb, nor hid sorrow from mine eyes.
options: Because it shut not up the doors of my mother’s womb, nor hid sorrow from mine eyes.

questions.question: What question did Job ask about his birth in Job 3:11?
questions.correct_answer: Why died I not from the womb?
options: Why died I not from the womb?

questions.question: What does Job 3:12 say?
questions.correct_answer: Why did the knees prevent me? or why the breasts that I should suck?
options: Why did the knees prevent me? or why the breasts that I should suck?

questions.question: What does Job 3:13 say?
questions.correct_answer: For now should I have lain still and been quiet, I should have slept: then had I been at rest,
options: For now should I have lain still and been quiet, I should have slept: then had I been at rest,

questions.question: What does Job 3:14 say?
questions.correct_answer: With kings and counsellors of the earth, which built desolate places for themselves;
options: With kings and counsellors of the earth, which built desolate places for themselves;

questions.question: What does Job 3:15 say?
questions.correct_answer: Or with princes that had gold, who filled their houses with silver:
options: Or with princes that had gold, who filled their houses with silver:

questions.question: What does Job 3:16 say?
questions.correct_answer: Or as an hidden untimely birth I had not been; as infants which never saw light.
options: Or as an hidden untimely birth I had not been; as infants which never saw light.

questions.question: What does Job 3:17 say?
questions.correct_answer: There the wicked cease from troubling; and there the weary be at rest.
options: There the wicked cease from troubling; and there the weary be at rest.

questions.question: What does Job 3:18 say?
questions.correct_answer: There the prisoners rest together; they hear not the voice of the oppressor.
options: There the prisoners rest together; they hear not the voice of the oppressor.

questions.question: What does Job 3:19 say?
questions.correct_answer: The small and great are there; and the servant is free from his master.
options: The small and great are there; and the servant is free from his master.

questions.question: What does Job 3:20 say?
questions.correct_answer: Wherefore is light given to him that is in misery, and life unto the bitter in soul;
options: Wherefore is light given to him that is in misery, and life unto the bitter in soul;

questions.question: What does Job 3:21 say?
questions.correct_answer: Which long for death, but it cometh not; and dig for it more than for hid treasures;
options: Which long for death, but it cometh not; and dig for it more than for hid treasures;

questions.question: What does Job 3:22 say?
questions.correct_answer: Which rejoice exceedingly, and are glad, when they can find the grave?
options: Which rejoice exceedingly, and are glad, when they can find the grave?

questions.question: What does Job 3:23 say?
questions.correct_answer: Why is light given to a man whose way is hid, and whom God hath hedged in?
options: Why is light given to a man whose way is hid, and whom God hath hedged in?

questions.question: What does Job 3:24 say?
questions.correct_answer: For my sighing cometh before I eat, and my roarings are poured out like the waters.
options: For my sighing cometh before I eat, and my roarings are poured out like the waters.

questions.question: What does Job 3:25 say?
questions.correct_answer: For the thing which I greatly feared is come upon me, and that which I was afraid of is come unto me.
options: For the thing which I greatly feared is come upon me, and that which I was afraid of is come unto me.

questions.question: What does Job 3:26 say?
questions.correct_answer: I was not in safety, neither had I rest, neither was I quiet; yet trouble came.
options: I was not in safety, neither had I rest, neither was I quiet; yet trouble came.

questions.question: What does Job 4:1 say?
questions.correct_answer: Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered and said,
options: Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered and said,

questions.question: What does Job 4:2 say?
questions.correct_answer: If we assay to commune with thee, wilt thou be grieved? but who can withhold himself from speaking?
options: If we assay to commune with thee, wilt thou be grieved? but who can withhold himself from speaking?

questions.question: What does Job 4:3 say?
questions.correct_answer: Behold, thou hast instructed many, and thou hast strengthened the weak hands.
options: Behold, thou hast instructed many, and thou hast strengthened the weak hands.

questions.question: What does Job 4:4 say?
questions.correct_answer: Thy words have upholden him that was falling, and thou hast strengthened the feeble knees.
options: Thy words have upholden him that was falling, and thou hast strengthened the feeble knees.

questions.question: What does Job 4:5 say?
questions.correct_answer: But now it is come upon thee, and thou faintest; it toucheth thee, and thou art troubled.
options: But now it is come upon thee, and thou faintest; it toucheth thee, and thou art troubled.

questions.question: What does Job 4:6 say?
questions.correct_answer: Is not this thy fear, thy confidence, thy hope, and the uprightness of thy ways?
options: Is not this thy fear, thy confidence, thy hope, and the uprightness of thy ways?

questions.question: What does Job 4:7 say?
questions.correct_answer: Remember, I pray thee, who ever perished, being innocent? or where were the righteous cut off?
options: Remember, I pray thee, who ever perished, being innocent? or where were the righteous cut off?

questions.question: What does Job 4:8 say?
questions.correct_answer: Even as I have seen, they that plow iniquity, and sow wickedness, reap the same.
options: Even as I have seen, they that plow iniquity, and sow wickedness, reap the same.

questions.question: What does Job 4:9 say?
questions.correct_answer: By the blast of God they perish, and by the breath of his nostrils are they consumed.
options: By the blast of God they perish, and by the breath of his nostrils are they consumed.

questions.question: What does Job 4:10 say?
questions.correct_answer: The roaring of the lion, and the voice of the fierce lion, and the teeth of the young lions, are broken.
options: The roaring of the lion, and the voice of the fierce lion, and the teeth of the young lions, are broken.

questions.question: What does Job 4:11 say?
questions.correct_answer: The old lion perisheth for lack of prey, and the stout lion’s whelps are scattered abroad.
options: The old lion perisheth for lack of prey, and the stout lion’s whelps are scattered abroad.

questions.question: What does Job 4:12 say?
questions.correct_answer: Now a thing was secretly brought to me, and mine ear received a little thereof.
options: Now a thing was secretly brought to me, and mine ear received a little thereof.

questions.question: What does Job 4:13 say?
questions.correct_answer: In thoughts from the visions of the night, when deep sleep falleth on men,
options: In thoughts from the visions of the night, when deep sleep falleth on men,

questions.question: What does Job 4:14 say?
questions.correct_answer: Fear came upon me, and trembling, which made all my bones to shake.
options: Fear came upon me, and trembling, which made all my bones to shake.

questions.question: What does Job 4:15 say?
questions.correct_answer: Then a spirit passed before my face; the hair of my flesh stood up:
options: Then a spirit passed before my face; the hair of my flesh stood up:

questions.question: What does Job 4:16 say?
questions.correct_answer: It stood still, but I could not discern the form thereof: an image was before mine eyes, there was silence, and I heard a voice, saying,
options: It stood still, but I could not discern the form thereof: an image was before mine eyes, there was silence, and I heard a voice, saying,

questions.question: What does Job 4:17 say?
questions.correct_answer: Shall mortal man be more just than God? shall a man be more pure than his maker?
options: Shall mortal man be more just than God? shall a man be more pure than his maker?

questions.question: What does Job 4:18 say?
questions.correct_answer: Behold, he put no trust in his servants; and his angels he charged with folly:
options: Behold, he put no trust in his servants; and his angels he charged with folly:

questions.question: What does Job 4:19 say?
questions.correct_answer: How much less in them that dwell in houses of clay, whose foundation is in the dust, which are crushed before the moth?
options: How much less in them that dwell in houses of clay, whose foundation is in the dust, which are crushed before the moth?

questions.question: What does Job 4:20 say?
questions.correct_answer: They are destroyed from morning to evening: they perish for ever without any regarding it.
options: They are destroyed from morning to evening: they perish for ever without any regarding it.

questions.question: What does Job 4:21 say?
questions.correct_answer: Doth not their excellency which is in them go away? they die, even without wisdom.
options: Doth not their excellency which is in them go away? they die, even without wisdom.

questions.question: What does Job 5:1 say?
questions.correct_answer: Call now, if there be any that will answer thee; and to which of the saints wilt thou turn?
options: Call now, if there be any that will answer thee; and to which of the saints wilt thou turn?

questions.question: What does Job 5:2 say?
questions.correct_answer: For wrath killeth the foolish man, and envy slayeth the silly one.
options: For wrath killeth the foolish man, and envy slayeth the silly one.

questions.question: What does Job 5:3 say?
questions.correct_answer: I have seen the foolish taking root: but suddenly I cursed his habitation.
options: I have seen the foolish taking root: but suddenly I cursed his habitation.

questions.question: What does Job 5:4 say?
questions.correct_answer: His children are far from safety, and they are crushed in the gate, neither is there any to deliver them.
options: His children are far from safety, and they are crushed in the gate, neither is there any to deliver them.

questions.question: What does Job 5:5 say?
questions.correct_answer: Whose harvest the hungry eateth up, and taketh it even out of the thorns, and the robber swalloweth up their substance.
options: Whose harvest the hungry eateth up, and taketh it even out of the thorns, and the robber swalloweth up their substance.

questions.question: What does Job 5:6 say?
questions.correct_answer: Although affliction cometh not forth of the dust, neither doth trouble spring out of the ground;
options: Although affliction cometh not forth of the dust, neither doth trouble spring out of the ground;

questions.question: What does Job 5:7 say?
questions.correct_answer: Yet man is born unto trouble, as the sparks fly upward.
options: Yet man is born unto trouble, as the sparks fly upward.

questions.question: What does Job 5:8 say?
questions.correct_answer: I would seek unto God, and unto God would I commit my cause:
options: I would seek unto God, and unto God would I commit my cause:

questions.question: What does Job 5:9 say?
questions.correct_answer: Which doeth great things and unsearchable; marvellous things without number:
options: Which doeth great things and unsearchable; marvellous things without number:

questions.question: What does Job 5:10 say?
questions.correct_answer: Who giveth rain upon the earth, and sendeth waters upon the fields:
options: Who giveth rain upon the earth, and sendeth waters upon the fields:

questions.question: What does Job 5:11 say?
questions.correct_answer: To set up on high those that be low; that those which mourn may be exalted to safety.
options: To set up on high those that be low; that those which mourn may be exalted to safety.

questions.question: What does Job 5:12 say?
questions.correct_answer: He disappointeth the devices of the crafty, so that their hands cannot perform their enterprise.
options: He disappointeth the devices of the crafty, so that their hands cannot perform their enterprise.

questions.question: What does Job 5:13 say?
questions.correct_answer: He taketh the wise in their own craftiness: and the counsel of the froward is carried headlong.
options: He taketh the wise in their own craftiness: and the counsel of the froward is carried headlong.

questions.question: What does Job 5:14 say?
questions.correct_answer: They meet with darkness in the daytime, and grope in the noonday as in the night.
options: They meet with darkness in the daytime, and grope in the noonday as in the night.

questions.question: What does Job 5:15 say?
questions.correct_answer: But he saveth the poor from the sword, from their mouth, and from the hand of the mighty.
options: But he saveth the poor from the sword, from their mouth, and from the hand of the mighty.

questions.question: What does Job 5:16 say?
questions.correct_answer: So the poor hath hope, and iniquity stoppeth her mouth.
options: So the poor hath hope, and iniquity stoppeth her mouth.

questions.question: What does Job 5:17 say?
questions.correct_answer: Behold, happy is the man whom God correcteth: therefore despise not thou the chastening of the Almighty:
options: Behold, happy is the man whom God correcteth: therefore despise not thou the chastening of the Almighty:

questions.question: What does Job 5:18 say?
questions.correct_answer: For he maketh sore, and bindeth up: he woundeth, and his hands make whole.
options: For he maketh sore, and bindeth up: he woundeth, and his hands make whole.

questions.question: What does Job 5:19 say?
questions.correct_answer: He shall deliver thee in six troubles: yea, in seven there shall no evil touch thee.
options: He shall deliver thee in six troubles: yea, in seven there shall no evil touch thee.

questions.question: What does Job 5:20 say?
questions.correct_answer: In famine he shall redeem thee from death: and in war from the power of the sword.
options: In famine he shall redeem thee from death: and in war from the power of the sword.

questions.question: What does Job 5:21 say?
questions.correct_answer: Thou shalt be hid from the scourge of the tongue: neither shalt thou be afraid of destruction when it cometh.
options: Thou shalt be hid from the scourge of the tongue: neither shalt thou be afraid of destruction when it cometh.

questions.question: What does Job 5:22 say?
questions.correct_answer: At destruction and famine thou shalt laugh: neither shalt thou be afraid of the beasts of the earth.
options: At destruction and famine thou shalt laugh: neither shalt thou be afraid of the beasts of the earth.

questions.question: What does Job 5:23 say?
questions.correct_answer: For thou shalt be in league with the stones of the field: and the beasts of the field shall be at peace with thee.
options: For thou shalt be in league with the stones of the field: and the beasts of the field shall be at peace with thee.

questions.question: What does Job 5:24 say?
questions.correct_answer: And thou shalt know that thy tabernacle shall be in peace; and thou shalt visit thy habitation, and shalt not sin.
options: And thou shalt know that thy tabernacle shall be in peace; and thou shalt visit thy habitation, and shalt not sin.

questions.question: What does Job 5:25 say?
questions.correct_answer: Thou shalt know also that thy seed shall be great, and thine offspring as the grass of the earth.
options: Thou shalt know also that thy seed shall be great, and thine offspring as the grass of the earth.

questions.question: What does Job 5:26 say?
questions.correct_answer: Thou shalt come to thy grave in a full age, like as a shock of corn cometh in in his season.
options: Thou shalt come to thy grave in a full age, like as a shock of corn cometh in in his season.

questions.question: What does Job 5:27 say?
questions.correct_answer: Lo this, we have searched it, so it is; hear it, and know thou it for thy good.
options: Lo this, we have searched it, so it is; hear it, and know thou it for thy good.

questions.question: What does Job 6:1 say?
questions.correct_answer: But Job answered and said,

questions.question: What does Job 6:2 say?
questions.correct_answer: Oh that my grief were throughly weighed, and my calamity laid in the balances together!
options: Oh that my grief were throughly weighed, and my calamity laid in the balances together!

questions.question: What does Job 6:3 say?
questions.correct_answer: For now it would be heavier than the sand of the sea: therefore my words are swallowed up.
options: For now it would be heavier than the sand of the sea: therefore my words are swallowed up.

questions.question: What does Job 6:4 say?
questions.correct_answer: For the arrows of the Almighty are within me, the poison whereof drinketh up my spirit: the terrors of God do set themselves in array against me.
options: For the arrows of the Almighty are within me, the poison whereof drinketh up my spirit: the terrors of God do set themselves in array against me.

questions.question: What does Job 6:5 say?
questions.correct_answer: Doth the wild ass bray when he hath grass? or loweth the ox over his fodder?
options: Doth the wild ass bray when he hath grass? or loweth the ox over his fodder?

questions.question: What does Job 6:6 say?
questions.correct_answer: Can that which is unsavoury be eaten without salt? or is there any taste in the white of an egg?
options: Can that which is unsavoury be eaten without salt? or is there any taste in the white of an egg?

questions.question: What does Job 6:7 say?
questions.correct_answer: The things that my soul refused to touch are as my sorrowful meat.
options: The things that my soul refused to touch are as my sorrowful meat.

questions.question: What does Job 6:8 say?
questions.correct_answer: Oh that I might have my request; and that God would grant me the thing that I long for!
options: Oh that I might have my request; and that God would grant me the thing that I long for!

questions.question: What does Job 6:9 say?
questions.correct_answer: Even that it would please God to destroy me; that he would let loose his hand, and cut me off!
options: Even that it would please God to destroy me; that he would let loose his hand, and cut me off!

questions.question: What does Job 6:10 say?
questions.correct_answer: Then should I yet have comfort; yea, I would harden myself in sorrow: let him not spare; for I have not concealed the words of the Holy One.
options: Then should I yet have comfort; yea, I would harden myself in sorrow: let him not spare; for I have not concealed the words of the Holy One.

questions.question: What does Job 6:11 say?
questions.correct_answer: What is my strength, that I should hope? and what is mine end, that I should prolong my life?
options: What is my strength, that I should hope? and what is mine end, that I should prolong my life?

questions.question: What does Job 6:12 say?
questions.correct_answer: Is my strength the strength of stones? or is my flesh of brass?
options: Is my strength the strength of stones? or is my flesh of brass?

questions.question: What does Job 6:13 say?
questions.correct_answer: Is not my help in me? and is wisdom driven quite from me?
options: Is not my help in me? and is wisdom driven quite from me?

questions.question: What does Job 6:14 say?
questions.correct_answer: To him that is afflicted pity should be shewed from his friend; but he forsaketh the fear of the Almighty.
options: To him that is afflicted pity should be shewed from his friend; but he forsaketh the fear of the Almighty.

questions.question: What does Job 6:15 say?
questions.correct_answer: My brethren have dealt deceitfully as a brook, and as the stream of brooks they pass away;
options: My brethren have dealt deceitfully as a brook, and as the stream of brooks they pass away;

questions.question: What does Job 6:16 say?
questions.correct_answer: Which are blackish by reason of the ice, and wherein the snow is hid:
options: Which are blackish by reason of the ice, and wherein the snow is hid:

questions.question: What does Job 6:17 say?
questions.correct_answer: What time they wax warm, they vanish: when it is hot, they are consumed out of their place.
options: What time they wax warm, they vanish: when it is hot, they are consumed out of their place.

questions.question: What does Job 6:18 say?
questions.correct_answer: The paths of their way are turned aside; they go to nothing, and perish.
options: The paths of their way are turned aside; they go to nothing, and perish.

questions.question: What does Job 6:19 say?
questions.correct_answer: The troops of Tema looked, the companies of Sheba waited for them.
options: The troops of Tema looked, the companies of Sheba waited for them.

questions.question: What does Job 6:20 say?
questions.correct_answer: They were confounded because they had hoped; they came thither, and were ashamed.
options: They were confounded because they had hoped; they came thither, and were ashamed.

questions.question: What does Job 6:21 say?
questions.correct_answer: For now ye are nothing; ye see my casting down, and are afraid.
options: For now ye are nothing; ye see my casting down, and are afraid.

questions.question: What does Job 6:22 say?
questions.correct_answer: Did I say, Bring unto me? or, Give a reward for me of your substance?
options: Did I say, Bring unto me? or, Give a reward for me of your substance?

questions.question: What does Job 6:23 say?
questions.correct_answer: Or, Deliver me from the enemy’s hand? or, Redeem me from the hand of the mighty?
options: Or, Deliver me from the enemy’s hand? or, Redeem me from the hand of the mighty?

questions.question: What does Job 6:24 say?
questions.correct_answer: Teach me, and I will hold my tongue: and cause me to understand wherein I have erred.
options: Teach me, and I will hold my tongue: and cause me to understand wherein I have erred.

questions.question: What does Job 6:25 say?
questions.correct_answer: How forcible are right words! but what doth your arguing reprove?
options: How forcible are right words! but what doth your arguing reprove?

questions.question: What does Job 6:26 say?
questions.correct_answer: Do ye imagine to reprove words, and the speeches of one that is desperate, which are as wind?
options: Do ye imagine to reprove words, and the speeches of one that is desperate, which are as wind?

questions.question: What does Job 6:27 say?
questions.correct_answer: Yea, ye overwhelm the fatherless, and ye dig a pit for your friend.
options: Yea, ye overwhelm the fatherless, and ye dig a pit for your friend.

questions.question: What does Job 6:28 say?
questions.correct_answer: Now therefore be content, look upon me; for it is evident unto you if I lie.
options: Now therefore be content, look upon me; for it is evident unto you if I lie.

questions.question: What does Job 6:29 say?
questions.correct_answer: Return, I pray you, let it not be iniquity; yea, return again, my righteousness is in it.
options: Return, I pray you, let it not be iniquity; yea, return again, my righteousness is in it.

questions.question: What does Job 6:30 say?
questions.correct_answer: Is there iniquity in my tongue? cannot my taste discern perverse things?
options: Is there iniquity in my tongue? cannot my taste discern perverse things?

questions.question: What does Job 7:1 say?
questions.correct_answer: Is there not an appointed time to man upon earth? are not his days also like the days of an hireling?
options: Is there not an appointed time to man upon earth? are not his days also like the days of an hireling?

questions.question: What does Job 7:2 say?
questions.correct_answer: As a servant earnestly desireth the shadow, and as an hireling looketh for the reward of his work:
options: As a servant earnestly desireth the shadow, and as an hireling looketh for the reward of his work:

questions.question: What does Job 7:3 say?
questions.correct_answer: So am I made to possess months of vanity, and wearisome nights are appointed to me.
options: So am I made to possess months of vanity, and wearisome nights are appointed to me.

questions.question: What does Job 7:4 say?
questions.correct_answer: When I lie down, I say, When shall I arise, and the night be gone? and I am full of tossings to and fro unto the dawning of the day.
options: When I lie down, I say, When shall I arise, and the night be gone? and I am full of tossings to and fro unto the dawning of the day.

questions.question: What does Job 7:5 say?
questions.correct_answer: My flesh is clothed with worms and clods of dust; my skin is broken, and become loathsome.
options: My flesh is clothed with worms and clods of dust; my skin is broken, and become loathsome.

questions.question: What does Job 7:6 say?
questions.correct_answer: My days are swifter than a weaver’s shuttle, and are spent without hope.
options: My days are swifter than a weaver’s shuttle, and are spent without hope.

questions.question: What does Job 7:7 say?
questions.correct_answer: O remember that my life is wind: mine eye shall no more see good.
options: O remember that my life is wind: mine eye shall no more see good.

questions.question: What does Job 7:8 say?
questions.correct_answer: The eye of him that hath seen me shall see me no more: thine eyes are upon me, and I am not.
options: The eye of him that hath seen me shall see me no more: thine eyes are upon me, and I am not.

questions.question: What does Job 7:9 say?
questions.correct_answer: As the cloud is consumed and vanisheth away: so he that goeth down to the grave shall come up no more.
options: As the cloud is consumed and vanisheth away: so he that goeth down to the grave shall come up no more.

questions.question: What does Job 7:10 say?
questions.correct_answer: He shall return no more to his house, neither shall his place know him any more.
options: He shall return no more to his house, neither shall his place know him any more.

questions.question: What does Job 7:11 say?
questions.correct_answer: Therefore I will not refrain my mouth; I will speak in the anguish of my spirit; I will complain in the bitterness of my soul.
options: Therefore I will not refrain my mouth; I will speak in the anguish of my spirit; I will complain in the bitterness of my soul.
